pages.raw_text:
The Central Labor union voted to
take Natatorium park off the UNI
fair list of that body last night after
a stormy session. The report OF
the committee appointed last Mon
day to investigate the lease held by
the lngersol Amusement Co. report
ed in favor of this action.


Delegate Lundy, of the electrical
workers, said he had a number of
contracts for wiring and electrical
work at the park and as his union
was the organization which origin
ated the boycott and a number Of
its members are employed there the
delegates from other unions voted
to open the park to everyone.


Considerable dissatisfaction was
expressed at the action of the cleo
trical workers and motion to ask
that Mr. Lundy be withdrawn from
the central body by his union was
literally seconded. but President
Maloney refused to put the motion.
The withdrawal of Lundy was ask
ed on the grounds that he is con-
tractor and ineligible TO seat in
the central body.


II was stated that the w. w. P
Co. would meet with a committee
from the Central Labor union and
the Structural Trades alliance any
time after Wednesday for the pur-
pose of making a settlement of the
differences now existing between
the company and the unions, and a
committee of was appointed by
the Central Labor union for this
conference.


That the company will settle
with the unions is doubted by
many members of organized labor.
1t is stated that the Musicians
union has been given until We'd
nesday to sign up 10 play at the
park. and unless this is done an
Italian band is to be secured TO


furnish music this summer. There
is no lack of labor men who say
that the offer to meet committees
after Wednesday is ruse and will
amount to nothing if the musicians
agree TO play.


However, ii Is certain the musi-
clans will play at the park. This
was settled by the action OF the
Central Labor union last night, 1t
Is the intention of the unions to
keep the lines of the company on
the unfair list. but it is doubtful if
such a boycott can be made eff
fective.
